div.p: TO ALL sons of Holy Mother Church present and future Isabel wife of William of Ryedale sends greeting. Know me to have given and granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and the monks serving God in that place, for the salvation of the lord Patrick of Ryedale, and Walter his son, and William my spouse, and for my own salvation, and for the souls of all our ancestors and successors, the oxgang of land in the territory of Whitton that my father William, the parson of Hownam, bought from Geoffrey the cook, and donated to me along with the charter of the foresaid Geoffrey and the confirmation of Patrick of Ryedale together, by these boundaries, as the same oxgang of land falls between Hardlaw and Shielstockbraes, and between the fosse of Harrow and the dower-land of Matilda Corbet, and as the course of the ditch goes around it by the foresaid boundaries. I have given this whole land and whatever is contained within the foresaid boundaries to the same monks in free, pure, and perpetual alms and released from every earthly service, custom, and secular exaction, that they may have, hold and possess it for ever, as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as they hold or possess any alms freely, quitely, fully, and honourably. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant this alms to the foresaid monks against all men, and acquit it of every service for ever. And that this my donation should be firm and fixed I have sworn by holding the holy gospels in the presence of the church before worthy men; and neither I, nor any of my heirs, shall seek to challenge it by art or cunning from which the house of Melrose could in future incur any loss or injury in respect of the same land. As witnesses, the lord Patrick of Ryedale; Walter of Ryedale, his son; William my spouse; William our son; Patrick son of Walter of Ryedale; Ralph of Ryedale; Robert of Barnoldby; Alexander the clerk; Reginald the parson's man; Adam of Huntly; Henry de Heriz; Walter son of Geoffrey Cook; Geoffrey son of Henry of Newbattle; William son of Mabilla; William nephew of the lord Patrick of Ryedale; and many others.
